master_row.title: (μ ∝ M R^2Ω) Universal Scaling Law
master_row.canonical_statement: The magnetic moment scales with the product of mass, square of size, and rotation rate across 47 orders of magnitude, indicating a deep link between inertial and magnetic properties.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation relating Ω, k, M, R.
master_row.dictionary: The magnetic moment () of a rotating body obeys ( = k,M,R^2,), where (M) is mass, (R) the characteristic radius, () the angular velocity, and (k) a universal proportionality constant determined by the Trawin base unit.
master_row.encyclopedia: The magnetic moment is modeled as proportional to mass, squared radius, and angular velocity, with $k$ encoding the Trawinistic coupling scale. Interpretive role: This law turns the Elsasser and helicity story into a concrete observable prediction. It explains why bodies spanning wide ranges of scale can still be compared on a single magnetic-moment axis once their rotational geometry is normalized.
